title: gnaphalium uliginosum subsp. nudum
intro: Gnaphalium uliginosum subsp. nudum, also known as Marsh Cudweed or Nudum Cudweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Gnaphalium uliginosum subsp. nudum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant may be found in tea, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
